description: Phase 10 for mealplan: add CLI and application golden regression tests, packaging/installability verification, and release-readiness checklist plus CI gates.

userStories.title: Add CLI golden snapshot regression suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want golden snapshots for critical CLI outputs so user-facing behavior regressions are caught immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Use `pytest` with file-based golden fixtures stored under `tests/golden/cli/` (no additional snapshot plugin required).
Add golden tests for explicit CLI scenarios: successful default JSON output, successful `--format text`, successful `--format table`.

Include golden failure-mode scenarios: missing required option (`--age`), invalid enum value (`--gender invalid`), invalid format value (`--format xml`), invalid training-zones JSON, concise default runtime error output, and debug runtime traceback output.
Snapshots assert canonical structure and ordering for stable CLI behavior.
Snapshot tests are deterministic across repeated local and CI runs.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add application golden snapshot regression suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want golden snapshots of application responses so orchestration and domain regressions are detected independently from CLI formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: Use file-based golden fixtures under `tests/golden/application/` for `MealPlanCalculationService` outputs.
Cover explicit service scenarios: periodized day with training session, non-periodized day with training session, and omitted training session (`training_session=None`).
Snapshots validate canonical meal ordering and response shape consistency.
Application snapshot suite runs without CLI rendering dependencies.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement hybrid snapshot tolerance policy
userStories.description: As a test author, I want strict structural snapshots and explicit numeric tolerance handling so tests remain stable without masking real regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Define and document strict-vs-tolerant fields in a contributor-facing section in `README.md` or `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`.
Enforce strict checks for payload keys, canonical ordering, and string or enum values.
Apply tolerance only to numeric macro and energy fields (`TDEE`, `training_carbs_g`, `protein_g`, `carbs_g`, `fat_g`, and meal macro grams) using absolute tolerance `0.01`.
Centralize tolerance constants in one test helper module (for example `tests/golden/helpers.py`) and reuse it across CLI and application golden tests.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Verify package artifact builds
userStories.description: As a release owner, I want reproducible package build artifacts so the project can be distributed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add automated build verification for both source distribution (`sdist`) and wheel artifacts.
Build outputs are generated via `uv build` and placed in `dist/`.
Generated artifacts include expected package metadata and CLI entry points.
Packaging build checks are included in CI.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Verify pip-installable and runnable workflow
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean install-and-run path so I can use the tool outside the source tree.
acceptanceCriteria: Document and test isolated install workflow: create fresh venv, install `dist/*.whl` via pip, then execute smoke commands.
Verify installed command invocation for both `mealplan --help` and `python -m mealplan --help`.
Include at least one post-install smoke command validation.
Installability checks are automated in CI.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add release checklist documentation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a concrete release checklist so each release candidate is validated consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a release-readiness checklist document with pre-release gates.
Checklist includes quality gates, snapshot pass criteria, packaging checks, and install smoke checks.
Checklist includes versioning and release-note expectations for first usable release.
Checklist references exact commands and expected outcomes.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add CI automation for release-readiness gates
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI automation for snapshot, packaging, and install smoke checks so regressions are blocked before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs CLI and application golden snapshot suites.
CI builds both `sdist` and wheel artifacts.
CI runs install-and-smoke checks in an isolated environment.
CI is structured into separate jobs: quality/tests, packaging build, and install-smoke (with explicit dependencies between jobs).
CI failure diagnostics clearly identify snapshot drift, packaging failures, and install failures.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update docs and examples for release-ready usage
userStories.description: As a user, I want up-to-date usage and packaging docs so installation and command execution are unambiguous.
acceptanceCriteria: Update README and docs with packaged installation and execution paths.
Document deterministic-output and golden-testing expectations for contributors.
Add or refresh representative release-context CLI usage examples.
Ensure docs align with CI gates and release checklist workflow.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
